items.title: Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement

items.title: Inside Zcash's new node that targets Visa-scale privacy at 50,000 transactions per second
items.description: The new Zakura client is the first live piece of a plan to take Zcash from roughly one private transaction per second to payment-network scale.

items.title: France orders country's internet service providers to block Polymarket
items.description: The regulator cited concerns over addictive mechanics, a lack of self-exclusion tools, and a high volume of French users bypassing previous financial restrictions.

items.title: Crypto executives say digital native generations may never need a bank account
items.description: Steakhouse Financial co-founder Adrian Cachinero says digital-native generations may rely less on banks, while Binance says younger users are already driving crypto adoption in emerging markets.

items.title: DOG Mode explains Bitcoin's next governance fight
items.description: Leonidas' DOG Mode client challenges Bitcoin's default relay policies, reopening a philosophical debate over censorship, free markets and who really governs the network.

items.title: Trump targets Brazil's payments system while dollar stablecoins are quietly overtaking country's payments
items.description: Washington views Brazil’s promotion of non-dollar payment channels, including Pix and the growth of stablecoins, as a potential threat to dollar-based trade, even as dollar-linked stablecoins account for about 90% of Brazil's crypto transactions.

items.title: Here is why a massive $1.6 billion in crypto liquidity is sitting idle and wasting away
items.description: About $542 million weekly sat outside active trading ranges, meaning this capital earned zero fees and provided no market depth.

items.title: Massive bitcoin call spreads target $72,000 by month end, right when the Fed meets
items.description: Large traders are betting on a BTC price rise to $72,000 by the end of the month, latest options market flow suggests.

items.title: Tokenization has become a strategic priority for 84% of financial firms
items.description: A Broadridge survey found Wall Street is accelerating tokenization efforts while betting on hybrid markets where digital and traditional assets coexist.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Kaspersky identifies malware framework targeting crypto investors
items.description: Cybersecurity company Kaspersky said a newly identified malware framework is targeting cryptocurrency investors through social engineering tactics and trojanized GitHub apps.

items.title: French gambling regulator orders ISPs to block Polymarket
items.description: France’s gambling authority has ordered the country’s internet service providers to geoblock Polymarket, citing illegal gambling and market manipulation concerns.

items.title: FTX to distribute $900M to creditors in fifth payment round
items.description: The FTX Recovery Trust and company have distributed about $10 billion since the exchange filed for bankruptcy in November 2022, leaving users cut off from their funds.

items.title: Galaxy lands 15-year Texas Tech stadium naming rights deal
items.description: Galaxy Digital will rename Texas Tech’s football stadium under a 15-year agreement, expanding its West Texas presence as the state attracts growing crypto investment.

items.title: Consensys unknowingly outsourced developer work to North Korean
items.description: Through an introduction with a “reputable third-party service provider,“ the company took on a developer who, as part of an investigation, was revealed to be tied to North Korea.

items.title: Crypto Biz: When dollars disappear, stablecoins step in
items.description: Bolivia moves to recognize USDT amid a dollar shortage, while Bitcoin miners’ AI ambitions face fresh investor scrutiny.

items.title: OKX Europe lets users convert USDT to MiCA-compliant USDC
items.description: The feature offers European customers a voluntary path away from Tether’s USDT as MiCA rules reshape the region’s stablecoin market.

items.title: Two U.S. service members killed in Jordan, one missing; Iran threatens 'unforgettable lessons' after calling off interim peace agreement
items.description: When asked about Iran no longer abiding by the interim peace agreement, U.S. President Donald Trump reportedly said, "I couldn't care less."

items.title: World Cup audience stayed bipartisan despite Trump's prominent role, CNBC survey finds
items.description: Nearly half of registered voters watched the 2026 World Cup, with income and education shaping viewership more than party affiliation, the survey found.

items.title: Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce in 27 states due to cyclosporiasis outbreak
items.description: Taylor Farms is recalling iceberg lettuce in 27 states because of the potential that the greens could be contaminated with cyclosporiasis.

items.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
items.description: Investment firms are readying for an era with less public commentary from the U.S. central bank. Some are relying on AI for an edge.

items.title: Why Dana White thinks much more of UFC's future will be Power Slap and boxing
items.description: UFC president and CEO Dana White predicted that Power Slap may become as big as his flagship fighting sport at the Game Plan conference in New York.

items.title: Analysis: Fed Chairman Warsh faces an inflation credibility test after Congress hearings
items.description: The new Fed chairman avoided major stumbles in two days of testimony before the House and Senate, but faces a rapid test of his commitment to price stability.

items.title: Two unexpected revelations in Warren Buffett's CNBC interview
items.description: Warren Buffett tells CNBC that Bill Gates' ties to Jeffrey Epstein didn't drive his termination of donations.

items.title: Fanatics CEO Michael Rubin on two sports businesses his multibillion-dollar conglomerate won't enter
items.description: Fanatics has become one of the most influential companies in sports, building out a massive merchandise, collectibles and sports gambling platform.

items.description: Policymakers expected to prioritise spurring high-tech rather than ‘big bang’ for consumption, analysts say

items.title: How Maga fell hard for the gender binary
items.description: Pete Hegseth’s championing of testosterone for troops is the latest manifestation of the rightwing movement’s aesthetic

items.title: Heroes, villains and clowns: Simon Kuper’s World Cup awards
items.description: With Fifa a bit short on credibility, the FT columnist has stepped in to create his own prizes

items.title: Why ‘rightwing’ is no longer such a dirty word in Britain
items.description: When words like fascist and Nazi are thrown around, being merely conservative seems mild by comparison

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Uot-zXCpyBE! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: ‘Respect is important’: If my financial adviser said, ‘Hey,’ my wife and I would walk out. Where do you draw the line?
items.description: “I met with a financial representative who was not aware that AT&T was the shortened form of American Telephone and Telegraph.”

items.title: ‘They are relentless’: A scammer is trying to change the email on my empty Robinhood account. What do they have to gain?
items.description: “I’ve just been ignoring their attempts.”

items.title: Netflix is spending big money on sports. Is the company making the right bets?
items.description: The streaming service says live programming helps draw in new subscribers, but investors have become disillusioned with Netflix’s broader engagement trends.

items.title: Your Netflix bill is up 29% in just over a year. It’s time for Washington to step in.
items.description: Netflix is still a Wall Street favorite — and a target for government regulators

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kJ5XRl7PWxM! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: US strikes Iran after two American troops are killed in Jordan
items.description: Battle for control of Strait of Hormuz escalates after Tehran launches attack on Gulf states

items.title: No longer grim up north for UK banks as Burnham comes to power
items.description: NatWest and Lloyds push regional lending credentials as they look to finance new Labour leader’s devolution agenda

items.title: How Maga fell hard for the gender binary
items.description: Pete Hegseth’s championing of testosterone for troops is the latest manifestation of the rightwing movement’s aesthetic

items.title: Chinese leaders zero in on need for stimulus for economy
items.description: Policymakers expected to prioritise spurring high-tech rather than ‘big bang’ for consumption, analysts say

items.title: Xpeng says China close to building ‘killer’ rival to Tesla Model Y
items.description: Elon Musk’s SUV remains top-selling electric vehicle in many parts of the world as well as second most popular in China

items.title: Can an ‘unelected’ prime minister succeed?
items.description: Andy Burnham will take power without winning an election. So have most other recent British prime ministers

items.title: Why ‘rightwing’ is no longer such a dirty word in Britain
items.description: When words like fascist and Nazi are thrown around, being merely conservative seems mild by comparison

items.title: The art of knowing when to step down
items.description: Only a few master one of the hardest career tricks of them all

items.title: Adidas Hopes Its World Cup Moment Can Drive U.S. Sales
items.description: Nearly everything on the field on Sunday will bear the German company’s logo. Bjørn Gulden, the chief executive, says he ‘couldn’t have scripted it better.’

items.title: Madison Square Garden Sues Wired Magazine Over L.G.B.T.Q. Tracking Report
items.description: The Manhattan venue claims a July article from the technology magazine falsely implied that it tracked information about some celebrities’ sexual orientation for “discriminatory purposes.”

items.title: America Caught World Cup Fever. His Job Is to Capitalize on It.
items.description: Don Garber, the M.L.S. commissioner, has been planning for this moment for eight years.

items.title: World Cup Final Drives Billions in Bets on Kalshi and Polymarket
items.description: The soccer tournament has helped propel the prediction markets to new user records, creating what may be the largest gambling event in history.

items.title: Robinhood’s Chief on Trump Accounts and Why Trading Isn’t Necessarily Gambling
items.description: Vlad Tenev, the chief executive of the digital brokerage Robinhood, is trying to win over the next generation of Americans with Trump accounts.

items.title: Russia Bill Broadens Sanctions but Could Fuel Fears Over the Dollar
items.description: The Trump administration has recently sought to scale back sanctions as adversaries develop workarounds that some believe could erode the U.S. dollar’s supremacy.

items.title: What Will You Do in Retirement? Your Money Depends on It.
items.description: Workers often focus solely on their retirement savings balance. But figuring out how you want to spend the rest of your life could be job No. 1.

items.title: Why Locals Are Gatekeeping Their Favorite Places From Tourists
items.description: As visitors flock to places like Mexico City, Milan and Copenhagen, residents are rethinking what they share, and who they share it with.
